name: Buttermilk Cider Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 Granny Smith apple, core removed, cut into 8 wedges
1 Red Delicious Apple, core removed, cut into 8 wedges
3/4 cup Pecans, coarsely chopped and toasted
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chicken in a single layer in a glass baking dish.
2. Pour buttermilk over the chicken. Turn to coat. Cover; chill overnight.
3. Melt butter in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Add apples and sauté until golden, about 5 minutes.
4. Transfer apples to a bowl. Add cider to the same skillet. Reduce to 1/4 cup by boiling for about 8 minutes.
5. Add broth and cream. Continue boiling until reduced to sauce consistency, about 15 minutes.
- Apples and sauce can be made up to 1 day ahead. Cover separately and refrigerate.
6. Drain chicken, then coat generously with seasoned flour.
7. Heat oil in a large heavy skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5 minutes per side.
8. Transfer chicken to plates. Return apples to the skillet and reheat gently.
9. Divide sauce among plates. Stir in chives and season with salt and pepper.
10. Pour sauce over chicken. Sprinkle with toasted pecans.
